_note: Emotional-arc Reel for the Fi collar campaign. Problem (stress of losing your dog) → solution (Fi GPS). This seed carries an inline ig_caption field because product campaigns don't flow through generate_recipe — the caption is pre-written and brand-validated rather than LLM-generated.
ig_caption:
The longest 3 minutes of my life as a dog owner: Nalla slipping out a gate while I was unpacking the car.

• She came back on her own. We got lucky. A lot of dogs don't.
• Bluetooth trackers don't help when your dog is more than a living-room away
• Real GPS + nationwide LTE is what actually works when it counts

Comment SAFE and I'll DM you the full comparison plus the exact one we ended up putting on her collar.

What's the scariest close-call you've had with your dog?

#dogsafety #gpstracker #doglife #nallasdad #dogfoodandfun
title: The 3 Minutes We Thought We Lost Nalla

slides.prompt: Cinematic silhouette photography, 9:16 portrait, of a medium-sized mixed-breed dog standing alone on a grassy hilltop at dusk, backlit by warm amber evening light glowing behind the horizon. Long dramatic shadow stretching across the grass. Moody, emotional, slightly anxious composition — the dog looking out into the distance. Deep blue twilight sky above, golden horizon behind, film-grain quality. Shot on 50mm at f/2.8 with shallow depth of field. Absolutely no text, no watermarks, no logos, no visible collar, no GPS devices, no people, no captions overlaid.

overlay.headline:
MOST DOGS THAT
GO MISSING
overlay.subcopy: don't come back the same day.

slides.prompt: Cinematic close-up photography, 9:16 portrait, overhead 45-degree angle of a partially-open weathered wooden garden gate set in a stone wall, a single empty plain leather collar resting on gravel just inside the gate, one wooden shoe and a watering can in the background softly out of focus. Warm afternoon side-light from the right casting long shadows, quiet domestic foreboding mood. Film-grain, naturalistic, generous negative space. Shot on 50mm at f/2.8. No text anywhere, no watermarks, no logos, no brand marks, no people, no dogs visible, no written signs.

overlay.headline: ALL IT TAKES
overlay.subcopy: is an open gate and 10 seconds.

Warm indoor tungsten light on the hand mixed with fading blue twilight outside. Anxious, focused mood. Depth of field isolates the phone. Shot on 50mm at f/2.2. No text visible on the phone, no brand names or app icons, no people faces, no dogs, no written signs.

overlay.headline:
WE THOUGHT
SHE WAS GONE
overlay.subcopy: for the longest 3 minutes of my life.

slides.prompt: Cinematic eye-level photography, 9:16 portrait, of a medium-sized happy mixed-breed dog sitting relaxed on a wooden porch in warm golden-hour sunlight, wearing a simple minimal orange-toned smart collar with a small rounded charm hanging from it. The dog looks directly at the camera with calm trust, tongue slightly out. Warm cozy tones, domestic relief mood, gentle side-light from the right, soft shadows. Shot on 50mm at f/2.8 with focus on the dog, background softly out of focus showing the porch wood.
